name: Grafana OnCall
what_it_does: Grafana OnCall is an open-source incident response and on-call management platform with a REST API for managing schedules, escalation chains, alert groups, and integrations with monitoring sources.
use_cases: Query the current on-call engineer for a given schedule to route incident notifications to the right person automatically
Create and resolve alert groups programmatically to integrate custom alerting sources into the OnCall workflow
Manage escalation policies and notification channels via API to keep on-call rotations in sync with team changes
Retrieve incident history and alert group timelines to feed post-mortem analysis or AI-driven RCA workflows
Trigger manual pages or silences via API from runbooks or ChatOps bots during active incidents
not_for: Full incident management with ticketing, retrospectives, and status pages — OnCall is scoped to alerting and on-call, not complete incident lifecycle
Standalone deployment without Grafana — OnCall is tightly coupled to Grafana Cloud or a self-hosted Grafana instance
Teams that need mature enterprise features like audit logs and SSO without running Grafana infrastructure
best_when: You are already using Grafana for observability and want native on-call scheduling and alert routing without adopting PagerDuty or OpsGenie as a separate vendor.
avoid_when: You need a standalone incident management platform with built-in status pages, retrospective templates, and enterprise SSO that is independent of your monitoring stack.

auth.notes: API key passed as Authorization header; keys are scoped to an organisation. Grafana Cloud uses a different auth flow (Grafana service accounts) than self-hosted.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free and open-source for self-hosted; Grafana Cloud free tier includes OnCall with limited active alert groups
pricing.paid_tiers: Grafana Cloud Pro/Advanced tiers bundle OnCall; pricing based on overall Grafana Cloud usage
pricing.notes: Self-hosted is fully free under AGPL-3.0. Grafana Cloud managed offering has usage-based pricing tied to the broader Grafana Cloud plan.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented for self-hosted; Grafana Cloud enforces plan-level limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors return JSON with detail field; Grafana Cloud and self-hosted sometimes return different error shapes for the same condition.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET and DELETE are idempotent; alert group creation is idempotent if the same alert UID is used; schedule and escalation updates are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted and Grafana Cloud base URLs differ significantly — agents must be configured for the specific deployment type
Alert group IDs are internal — agents must look up groups by alert UID or external source rather than constructing IDs
The API for self-hosted OSS lags behind Grafana Cloud features; some endpoints documented for Cloud do not exist on OSS builds
On-call schedule resolution (who is currently on call) requires a separate API call per schedule — there is no bulk 'current on-call' endpoint
Webhook payloads from OnCall integrations are not signed by default, making inbound webhook consumers vulnerable to spoofing without network controls

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted deployments inherit operator's TLS and network security; no built-in webhook signing; API keys lack scope granularity. SOC2 applies to Grafana Cloud only.
